sectionSubtitle: The tech interview world has transformed. AI tools, in-person returns, and new skill demands are reshaping how companies hire engineers.

cards.title: AI-Enabled Interviews
cards.description: Meta rolled out AI-enabled coding interviews where candidates use AI assistants. Canva insists on it. The focus is on how you work with AI, not whether you memorized algorithms.

cards.title: In-Person is Back
cards.description: On-site interviews rose from 24% (2022) to 38% (2025). Companies like Google brought back in-person rounds to combat AI-assisted cheating.

cards.title: New Skills in Demand
cards.description: AI literacy, prompt engineering awareness, debugging AI output, designing systems with LLM components. 240% growth in AI-native engineering roles in early 2025.

cards.title: System Design Evolved
cards.description: Now includes RAG pipelines, autonomous agents, LLM integration patterns. Expect questions about scalability vs. cost trade-offs with AI components.

cards.title: Harder Algorithmic Questions
cards.description: Big Tech is doubling down on harder problems to differentiate candidates who truly understand vs. those who lean on AI. Quality of reasoning matters more.

cards.title: Company Split
cards.description: Big Tech: harder problems + in-person. Startups: AI tools encouraged, real-world scenarios, pair programming with AI. Know your target.
